responses: 👋 Hello, Gentinian! I am Tibby, GTDLNHS' cutest chatbot. How may I help you today?

patterns: What grade levels does the school offer
responses:
🏫 GTDLNHS caters to students from Grade 7 to Grade 12.
📚 We follow the new curriculum aligned with DepEd guidelines.
🎓 Instead of the traditional strands, we offer the Strengthened Senior High School Program, preparing students for career pathways like:
- Bakery Operations
- Business and Entrepreneurship
- Creative and Multimedia Arts
- Culinary Arts
- Engineering
- Food and Beverage Services
- Health Sciences
- Hospitality and Tourism
- Social Science and Humanities
- Software Development

patterns: What is the address of the school?
How can I get to General Tiburcio de Leon National High School?
Location of General Tiburcio de Leon National High School
How do I find the school?
responses: 🐾 General Tiburcio de Leon National High School is located at Corner Mercado St., General T. de Leon, Valenzuela City, Metro Manila, Philippines.

patterns: What are the requirements for moving up or graduating?
What do I need to graduate?
What do I need to move up?
Are school projects and performance tasks required?
Do we need to submit projects?
Is Work Immersion required in SHS?
Do I have to do Work Immersion?
Is Work Immersion mandatory in Senior High School?
responses: 🐾 To move up, you must complete the required academic level and submit important documents such as your Certificate of Completion and Certificate of Good Moral Character. To graduate from Senior High School, you must complete all required subjects for both Grade 11 and Grade 12, and meet the school’s academic and behavioral standards.

🐾 Performance tasks are part of the grading system and are required in every subject. Projects, on the other hand, may vary depending on your teacher. Always check with your subject teachers for specific instructions and deadlines.
🐾 Yes, work immersion is a required component for graduation in the Senior High School (SHS) program. It's a mandatory course under the K to 12 curriculum.

patterns: How can I view my grades?
Where can I see my grades?
How do I check my grades?
What happens if I fail a subject?
What should I do if I fail a subject?
Can I transfer strands in SHS?
How do I change my SHS strand?
Is it possible to switch strands in Senior High School?
responses: 🐾 You can view your grades during the scheduled card distribution day. Your teachers and advisers will also be available to discuss your performance and answer any questions you may have. We encourage you to attend with a parent or guardian so everyone stays informed about your academic progress.

🐾 If you fail a subject, you will need to retake it to meet the academic requirements. Don’t worry—your teachers are here to support you, and there are always ways to improve and succeed with effort and commitment.

🐾 Yes, you may transfer to a different strand in Senior High School. However, you’ll need to take any subjects required in your new strand that you haven’t completed in your previous one. It's best to consult with the academic coordinator or your adviser to guide you through the process.

📝 To enroll, visit the Registrar’s Office (Building A) with:
- PSA Birth Certificate
- Report Card (SF9)
- Good Moral Certificate
🚫 No entrance exam is required.
✅ We accept transferees until the end of the 2nd quarter.

patterns: When does the school year start
responses:
📅 School year starts in June.
⏰ Hours vary by grade:
- Grade 7: 6:00 AM – 12:30 PM
- Grade 8: 12:40 PM – 7:00 PM
- Grade 9: 1:00 PM – 7:20 PM
- Grade 10: 6:00 AM – 12:40 PM
- Grade 11: 6:00 AM – 12:30 PM
- Grade 12: 12:45 PM – 7:30 PM
💡 Yes! We have a Special Education (SPED) Program to support students with special needs or disabilities.

🏫 GTDLNHS facilities include:
- Comfort Rooms
- Computer Laboratory
- Faculty Room
- Guidance Office
- Library
- Multi-purpose Hall
- School Canteen
- School Clinic
- Science Laboratory

🎯 We have many clubs and orgs to join, like:
- Ang Daluyan
- Ecosavers Club
- English Club
- ESP Club
- Junior’s Computers Club
- Language Club
- Library Club
- Math Club
- Nexus Club
- Science Club
- Sipnayan Club
- Siyensaya Club
- Social Science Club
- The Genzette
- TLE Club
💙 Our Guidance Office is here for academic, personal, career, and social concerns. Drop by anytime for support.

👕 Junior High: Uniform Mon–Thu, PE attire Fri.
👕 Senior High: PE attire on HOPE day, uniforms on other days.

☎️ Contact us at 0967-023-7047
📧 Email: gtdlnhs2007@gmail.com
📱 FB: https://www.facebook.com/share/1BMPoam8K2/?mibextid=wwXIfr
💻 We don’t offer full online or blended learning, but Open High School is available for students with health concerns.

📢 Class suspensions are announced via:
- School admin
- Local gov’t
- Official government channels
📜 Follow our Code of Conduct: respect, responsibility, and kindness. Breaking rules can result in suspension or expulsion.

patterns: Can I join a student organization?
Are there student clubs at GTDLNHS?
How can I run for SSG?
How do I join the Supreme Student Government?
responses: 🐾 Absolutely! Most clubs and organizations open membership at the start of the school year, so keep an eye out for announcements.
🐾 You may reach out to Sir Arnielson Calubiran or visit the SSLG headquarters to file your candidacy form.
🐾 Goodbye, Gentinian! Stay awesome and see you around the campus!
